description: Frozen v1 invariants for subject authority events and deterministic authority-history evaluation.

domains.meaning: An authority_event is a semantic-class assertion that claims authority to change a scoped authority graph.
Canonical subject state is derived from causally ordered authority history.
The same causally ordered authority history always produces the same canonical subject state.
Authority in one domain does not grant authority in another domain.
Legacy event bridging is loss-minimizing and must not generate new authority facts.

failure_taxonomy.meaning: Authority event violates the authority-event JSON Schema.
Object is not a valid authority_event semantic class.
Authority event subject is missing or not a valid subject address.
Authority domain is not recognized by the evaluator.
Action is not valid for the declared authority domain.
Authority event timestamp is missing or not a valid date-time.
Authority event proof type is missing or unsupported.
Authority event proof controller is missing or not a valid subject address.
Transfer authority event requires a recipient subject.
Capability grant authority event requires both capability and scope.
previous_state_reference does not match the evaluator state before applying the event.
History is not in valid causal order.
Projection or application view was supplied where authority history is required.
Bridge attempted to create authority facts not present in legacy event semantics.
Bridge refused to translate a legacy event type without accepted subject-authority semantics.

axioms: same causally ordered authority_history -> identical canonical_subject_state
bridge MUST be loss-minimizing, not truth-generating
projection input MUST NOT affect canonical subject state
device owner relation is scoped device authority, not Layer D asset ownership
The system never edits identity; it appends causality and re-evaluates subject state
